comments.body:
­ƒôØ Walkthrough ## Walkthrough The PR introduces a multi-agent collaboration protocol update ("The Loop" workflow: Repair Mission ÔåÆ Sonnet Handoff ÔåÆ Branch/Code/Push ÔåÆ PR Re-Audit Matrix ÔåÆ Merge) across configuration and manifest files. It consolidates guidance documents to reference a unified standards manifesto, adds GitHub Actions audit workflows, and augments the trading strategy codebase with extensive stateLock-based synchronization guards across entry, order callback, and position management code paths to prevent race conditions. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Manifesto & Standards**
`.agent/standards_manifesto.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`|Reworked multi-agent collaboration protocol introducing "The Loop" workflow, refined role descriptions (Lead Engineer, Bridge), expanded autonomy guidance with 5-step process, and unified guidance to reference the manifesto as single source of truth.| |**Configuration & Instructions**
`.claude.json`, `GEMINI.md`|Added new Claude custom instructions directive to follow manifesto standards; added Autonomous Repair Loop guidance under Zero-Trust / Repo Hygiene.| |**Worktree & Submodule References**
`.claude/worktrees/affectionate-franklin`, `.claude/worktrees/sleepy-jepsen`|Added subproject commit references to new worktree structures.| |**Audit & Reporting**
`codex_audit.md`, `pr24_audit_utf8.json`, `pr_checks.json`, `gh_log.txt`|Added comprehensive forensic audit report, PR audit artifacts with review metadata, PR check status snapshot, and GitHub Actions step logging for audit result posting.| |**GitHub Actions Workflows**
`prdiff.txt`, other workflow references|Configured new Codex Pre-Triage PR Audit and Gemini PR Auditor workflows to generate and post audit results as PR comments.| |**Entry Point Synchronization**
`src/V12_002.Entries.FFMA.cs`, `src/V12_002.Entries.MOMO.cs`, `src/V12_002.Entries.OR.cs`, `src/V12_002.Entries.RMA.cs`, `src/V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs`|Introduced stateLock-protected state updates to activePositions and entryOrders; added null-check abort guards on order submissions; delayed state mutation until after successful order validation to prevent TOCTOU race conditions.| |**Order Callback & Management**
`src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.cs`|Wrapped dictionary mutations (stopOrders, entryOrders, activePositions) in stateLock; consolidated multi-field state updates into atomic critical sections; added deferred cleanup logic for terminal scenarios; expanded synchronization to cover follower replacement FSM and execution update paths.| |**Specialized Handlers**
`src/V12_002.REAPER.cs`, `src/V12_002.SIMA.cs`, `src/V12_002.Symmetry.cs`, `src/V12_002.Trailing.cs`|Added in-flight repair markers and flatten-running guards in REAPER; added early abort and queue-drain logic in SIMA on disable; added grace window stamping and locked EntryFilled updates in Symmetry; introduced circuit-breaker logic (FlattenAttemptCount) and stateLock guards around stop order writes in Trailing.| |**Core Types & Build Configuration**
`src/V12_002.cs`|Added PendingCleanup and FlattenAttemptCount fields to PositionInfo; updated BUILD_TAG from "950" to "956" reflecting refactoring scope.| |**Test Infrastructure**
`test-vertex.js`|Added JavaScript test script to exercise Google Cloud Vertex AI generative models across multiple model variants and geographic locations.| ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant LE as Lead Engineer
(Claude/Sonnet) participant Branch as Branch
Operations participant PR as PR System participant Matrix as Re-Audit Matrix
(Opus/Gemini) participant Merge as Merge LE->>LE: Repair Mission
Identified LE->>LE: Sonnet Handoff
Ready LE->>Branch: Checkout
Branch LE->>Branch: Code
Implementation LE->>Branch: Push
Changes Branch->>PR: Create PR PR->>Matrix: Trigger Phase 3
Omni-Audit Matrix->>Matrix: Opus Reviews Matrix->>Matrix: Sonnet Reviews Matrix->>Matrix: Gemini Reviews Matrix->>PR: Post Review
Comments PR->>LE: Request
Human Sign-off LE->>Merge: Approve &
Merge Merge->>Merge: Integrated ``` ## Estimated code review effort ­ƒÄ» 4 (Complex) | ÔÅ▒´©Å ~50 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - mkalhitti-cloud/universal-or-strategy#29 ÔÇö Modifies the same entry state synchronization area (`src/V12_002.Entries.Retest.cs` activePositions/entryOrders writes with stateLock). ## Suggested labels `Orders / Callbacks`, `Core Strategy`, `Workflows / CI`, `Agent / Manifesto` ## Poem > ­ƒÉ░ *Hop-hop, sync-sync, the locks align,* > *The Loop now spins in perfect time,* > *Four agents dance, no race in sight,* > *Each state protected, held up right!* Ô£¿
­ƒÜÑ Pre-merge checks | Ô£à 2 | ÔØî 1 ### ÔØî Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ÔÜá´©Å Warning | Docstring coverage is 59.46% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
Ô£à Passed checks (2 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :---------------: | :------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | Ô£à Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbitÔÇÖs high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | Ô£à Passed | The title 'Build 956: Audit Remediation' is fully related to the main changes in the changeset and clearly summarizes the primary objective of implementing audit findings across concurrency, FSM, lifecycle, and core components. |
Ô£Å´©Å Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
Ô£¿ Finishing Touches - [ ] ­ƒôØ Generate docstrings (stacked PR) - [ ] ­ƒôØ Generate docstrings (commit on current branch)
­ƒº¬ Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Post copyable unit tests in a comment - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `build/956-audit-remediation`

DeepSource Code Review

We reviewed changes in 5763f19...9c86228 on this pull request. Below is the summary for the review, and you can see the individual issues we found as inline review comments.

See full review on DeepSource Ôåù

> [!IMPORTANT] > Some issues found as part of this review are outside of the diff in this pull request and aren't shown in the inline review comments due to GitHub's API limitations. You can see those issues on the DeepSource dashboard.

PR Report Card

Overall Grade  

Focus Area: Complexity 		Security  

Reliability  

Complexity  

Hygiene  

Feedback

  • Monolithic handler mixing concerns
    • The large HandleMatchedFollowerOrder bundles parsing, business rules and side-effects, inflating branches and duplicating logic; splitting into validation, decision, and effect layers (pure functions + thin orchestrator) will cut paths and ease testing.
  • Branch-driven logic instead of declarative rules
    • Many nested if/switches implement permutations; moving to table-driven rules, strategy objects or a state machine reduces case explosion and collapses cyclomatic complexity into data-driven evaluations.
  • Near-duplicate conditionals scattered across modules
    • Repeated occurrences suggest duplicated control logic across handlers; extract a single domain service with small, well-tested rule evaluators to stop copyÔÇæpaste and ensure fixes lower complexity globally.

Code Review Summary

Analyzer Status Updated (UTC) Details
C# Mar 9, 2026 2:17a.m. Review Ôåù
Secrets Mar 9, 2026 2:17a.m. Review Ôåù

### ­ƒñû Antigravity Auto-Auditor (Gemini 2.5 Pro via Vertex AI)

Greetings. I am the Gemini CLI, Antigravity Auto-Auditor. I have completed my analysis of this pull request against the `GEMINI.md` project standards.

### **Verdict: REJECTED**

This pull request introduces severe violations of project standards and cannot be approved. While the intent appears to be the addition of audit tooling and documentation, the execution is fundamentally flawed. The PR commits numerous temporary artifacts and log files, directly violating repo hygiene rules. Critically, it also includes a third-party audit report (`codex_audit.md`) that identifies multiple **Critical** safety violations in the codebase (specifically, state mutation without `lock(stateLock)`), but the PR itself makes **no corresponding C# source code changes** to fix them.

---

### **Systematic Review of Violations**

#### 1. Standard: Repo Hygiene & Binary Guard

**VIOLATION:** This PR commits a large number of temporary, generated, or artifact files. This is a direct violation of the `Binary Guard` rule ("DO NOT commit `.exe`, `.log`, or `.bak` files") and the general `Clean Dashboard` principle. The repository's source of truth must remain pristine.

The following committed files are in violation:
*   `/.claude/worktrees/affectionate-franklin` (git worktree metadata)
*   `/.claude/worktrees/sleepy-jepsen` (git worktree metadata)
*   `/codex_audit.md` (audit report artifact)
*   `/diff.txt` (binary/artifact)
*   `/gh_log.txt` (log file)
*   `/help.txt` (binary/artifact)
*   `/pr24_audit.json` (audit artifact)
*   `/pr24_audit_utf8.json` (audit artifact)
*   `/pr26_diff.txt` (binary/artifact)
*   `/pr_checks.json` (CI artifact)
*   `/prdiff.txt` (diff artifact)

**Required Action:** These files must be removed from the commit history. They should be added to `.gitignore` if they are a regular part of the development workflow. Audit reports are for PR comments and mission briefs, not for committing to `main`.

#### 2. Standard: Concurrency (`lock(stateLock)`)

**VIOLATION (by Omission):** This pull request adds the file `codex_audit.md`, which contains the following critical finding:

> **F1 | Critical | ... | `src/V12_002.Entries.FFMA.cs:172`, `...` | Confirmed: `activePositions`, `entryOrders`, `stopOrders`, and target dictionaries are still written and removed outside `lock(stateLock)`.**

This confirms the codebase is in direct violation of the most critical concurrency standard in `GEMINI.md`. However, a review of this PR's diff shows **zero modifications** to any C# source files (`src/*.cs`).

The `Autonomous Repair Loop` standard, which this PR adds, mandates that a fix mission should *implement* a repair. This PR only commits the diagnosis, leaving the critical bug unfixed.

**Required Action:** The purpose of an audit is to generate a mission to *fix* the findings. This PR must be closed. A new PR must be opened that implements the necessary `lock(stateLock)` guards around all shared state mutations as identified in the audit.

#### 3. Standard: MOVE-SYNC / Follower Order Replace Pattern

**VIOLATION (by Omission):** The `codex_audit.md` file also identifies violations of the mandatory FSM for follower order replacement.

> **F2 | Critical | ... | `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.cs:832-843` | Confirmed: follower CIT still does raw `Cancel()` -> `CreateOrder()` -> `Submit()` ...**
>
> **F4 | High | ... | Confirmed: several follower stop/target move paths still cancel and resubmit directly.**

This confirms that the ban on direct `Cancel()`+`Submit()` for follower orders is being violated. Again, this PR makes no C# changes to bring these paths into compliance with the two-phase replace FSM standard.

**Required Action:** The new repair PR must refactor these non-compliant follower replacement paths to use the required `_followerReplaceSpecs` FSM.

### **Conclusion**

This PR is non-compliant and unsafe to merge. It pollutes the repository with artifacts and fails to execute its implicit mission: fixing the critical bugs it documents.

**Recommendation to Director:**
1.  **Close** this pull request immediately.
2.  **Purge** the feature branch to remove the committed artifacts.
3.  **Initiate** a new `$MISSION` based on the findings in `codex_audit.md`.
4.  The new mission's resulting PR must contain **only** the necessary C# source code changes to address the `lock` and FSM violations, and must not contain any report or log files.

End of audit.

Greptile Summary

This PR (Build 956) implements the forensic audit remediations from `codex_audit.md` across the full `src/` surface. The core theme is consistent: move all `activePositions`, `entryOrders`, `stopOrders`, and `_followerReplaceSpecs` mutations behind `lock(stateLock)`; gate entry registration behind a null-abort check; defer `activePositions` teardown until the broker confirms stop terminal state (A2-2); and add a SIMA lifecycle gate and REAPER double-enqueue guard. The majority of individual fixes are clean and materially improve the concurrency safety of the strategy. However, two logic bugs were introduced in the dual-leg null-abort paths, and the A2-2 deferred cleanup blocks are incomplete: - **`linkedTRENDEntries` ghost keys on E1 abort** (`src/V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs`): When E1 `SubmitOrderUnmanaged` returns null, the function returns early but `linkedTRENDEntries[entry1Name]` and `linkedTRENDEntries[entry2Name]` ÔÇö written before the submit ÔÇö are never removed. Callback paths that iterate `linkedTRENDEntries` will find orphaned cross-link pairs on the next signal cycle. - **Partial rollback on E2 abort** (`src/V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs`): When E2 submit fails, E1 remains in `activePositions` and `entryOrders` while `linkedTRENDEntries[entry1Name]` still points to a ghost E2 name. Fill callbacks for E1 will attempt to process the missing E2 partner, leading to undefined cleanup behaviour for the linked leg. - **A2-2 deferred cleanup omits `stopOrders.TryRemove`** (`src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` lines 399ÔÇô421 and 681ÔÇô696): Both the master and follower deferred-purge blocks call `activePositions.TryRemove` and `SymmetryGuardForgetEntry` but never remove the corresponding `stopOrders` entry. The stop order reference (already terminal/cancelled) persists indefinitely and can trigger spurious re-submission attempts. - **`TotalContracts` read without `stateLock` in A2-3** (`src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` lines 632ÔÇô639): The follower cancel delta rollback reads `cancelledFollowerPos.TotalContracts` outside the lock, violating the project's own zero-trust concurrency contract. - **`.cursorrules` / `.github/copilot-instructions.md` content stripped**: Both files replaced detailed, actionable guardrails (ASCII-only strings, stateLock requirements, Auto-Reject conditions) with a single-line reference to the manifesto, removing direct context for AI code-review tools that read these files as their active instruction set.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- Not safe to merge ÔÇö the dual-leg null-abort paths leave ghost cross-link state that will corrupt callback processing on the next TREND/RMA signal cycle, and the A2-2 deferred cleanup blocks leave stop-order ghost references that can trigger spurious re-submission on closed positions. - The broad stateLock hardening and SIMA/REAPER lifecycle gating are sound improvements. However, two introduced logic bugs (`linkedTRENDEntries` leak on E1 abort, partial rollback on E2 abort) directly affect live trading state for the TREND and RMA entry modes, and the A2-2 ghost stop-order leak can cause the REAPER/callback layer to attempt to re-submit stops for positions that have already been fully closed. These are not edge-case scenarios ÔÇö any null return from `SubmitOrderUnmanaged` or any final-target fill followed by a stop cancel will exercise the broken paths. - `src/V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs` (E1 and E2 null-abort paths), `src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` (both A2-2 deferred-cleanup blocks at lines 399ÔÇô421 and 681ÔÇô696).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | src/V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs | Applies A1-1/A2-1 null-abort + stateLock fixes but introduces two partial-rollback bugs: E1 abort returns early without clearing pre-populated linkedTRENDEntries, and E2 abort leaves E1 fully registered while its linked E2 key is orphaned. | | src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs | Large set of stateLock hardening and deferred A2-2 cleanup changes; A2-2 deferred purge blocks fail to remove the corresponding stopOrders entry, leaving ghost references after position teardown. | | src/V12_002.Entries.RMA.cs | Correctly gates E1 registration behind null check + stateLock; E2 null path logs but does not clean up the already-written linkedTRENDEntries, leaving cross-linked ghost keys when E2 submit fails. | | src/V12_002.Orders.Management.cs | stopOrders and dict mutations consistently moved inside stateLock; activePositions removal and pendingStopReplacements cleanup atomically guarded. No significant new issues introduced. | | src/V12_002.Trailing.cs | stopOrders writes moved inside stateLock; A3-3 circuit breaker added to cap emergency flatten attempts; FlattenAttemptCount reset on success is unguarded but is a low-risk plain-block access. | | src/V12_002.REAPER.cs | A3-2 moves _repairInFlight guard to enqueue time (before TriggerCustomEvent) and adds isFlattenRunning abort gate; change is logically sound. | | src/V12_002.SIMA.cs | A3-1 adds SIMA/flatten gate and dispatch queue drain to PumpFleetDispatch; queue also drained on SIMA disable. Straightforward and correct. | | .cursorrules | Replaced detailed security/concurrency/naming rules with a single-line reference to the manifesto, discarding critical inline guidance (ASCII-only strings, stateLock requirements, FSM rules, auto-reject conditions). | | .github/copilot-instructions.md | Stripped detailed Zero-Trust review checklist and Auto-Reject conditions in favour of a single-line manifesto reference; significant loss of AI-reviewer context and guardrails. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A["Entry Method\n(FFMA/OR/MOMO/TREND/RMA)"] --> B["CreateTRENDPosition / ApplyTargetLadderGuard"] B --> C{"TREND/RMA dual-leg?\n(linkedTRENDEntries set here)"} C -->|Yes| D["SubmitOrderUnmanaged E1"] C -->|No| E["SubmitOrderUnmanaged"] D --> F{E1 == null?} F -->|Yes| G["Roll back masterDeltaE1\nPrint ENTRY_ABORT\nreturn\nÔÜá´©Å linkedTRENDEntries NOT cleaned"] F -->|No| H["lock(stateLock)\nactivePositions + entryOrders = E1"] H --> I["SubmitOrderUnmanaged E2"] I --> J{E2 == null?} J -->|Yes| K["Roll back masterDeltaE2\nPrint ENTRY_ABORT\nreturn\nÔÜá´©Å E1 stays in dict, link to ghost E2"] J -->|No| L["lock(stateLock)\nactivePositions + entryOrders = E2"] E --> M{Order == null?} M -->|Yes| N["Roll back expectedPositions\nPrint ENTRY_ABORT\nreturn ÔÇö Clean"] M -->|No| O["lock(stateLock)\nactivePositions + entryOrders"] L --> P["SubmitBracketOrders on fill\nA2-2: set PendingCleanup on final target"] O --> P P --> Q{Stop terminal?} Q -->|Cancelled A2-2| R["lock(stateLock)\nactivePositions.TryRemove\nÔÜá´©Å stopOrders NOT removed"] Q -->|Filled| S["lock(stateLock)\nFull CleanupPosition"] ``` Last reviewed commit: 9c86228
Superseded by #32 (Build 957: Audit Remediation Loop 1) which addresses all remaining findings from Antigravity, CodeRabbit, and DeepSource audits. Closing this PR.
